This isn't intended as a response video. I intend to go into great detail about much of the misinformation being spread in an extensive video essay which I'll be working on separately from this one, to be released at a later date. I also plan to educate people on the lesser-known Minecraft mechanics behind some of the clips being used as evidence against me. Unrelated to "hacking", there have been a lot of verifiably false claims being cast towards me during these smear campaigns, which also need to be debunked. It is regularly claimed that the Tier List migration was a decision intended purely for self-benefit/fraudulence, despite substantial evidence justifying the necessity of splitting off. This alongside the allegations of theft, etc. will be included in my debunk video, but if you're interested in reading up on part of it, you can view the document I previously wrote regarding the split: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1OMeUHlRto_vwXPbWjzHZuj5Ab4IRk7Mtuzvq-b--X1Y

This montage is a special upload to me. With Vanilla being my main game mode, I have learned multiple valuable lessons throughout the years I've been playing it.
Thank you to everyone that has contributed to the Vanilla PvP community - I was so eager to finally upload this video that I edited it fully in just a day!

In 2023, I intended to release my 3 year montage, but was unable to largely due to file loss from malware being executed on my PC during one of my screenshares. The following year, I was dealing with other large-scale problems affecting the community, and ultimately decided it would be best if I waited until this year to finally make my next Vanilla PvP montage.

I intend on posting a couple more videos in the nearer future before re-focusing on some of the other projects I'm working on, so stay tuned.

*I've been receiving a LOT of backlash over this video, so I'd like to explain my point of view on this and why I publicized this mod:*
Contrary to what you may have thought, I'm actually quite against PVP-enhancement mods. They have been freely allowed in this community for a long time despite providing a distinct advantage over players not using said mods. I'd like to provide a few examples so that you can get a better understanding of my side of things.

_"Marlow, this mod provides an unfair advantage because players require less skill in laggy environments!"_

*Armor HUD* - a mod that many players use in PVP allows you to see your exact durability down to the point for all armor pieces while you are performing other actions such as moving around, attacking, etc. I've actually found Armor HUD to net a LARGE advantage especially when mending armor, since you can freely run around throwing XP bottles without having to stop moving/open your inventory and hover over your armor pieces. Imagine being in a fast-paced hole fight and you have to stop everything you're doing to check to make sure your armor isn't going to break while your opponent already knows the whole time while they are moving and attacking you. It's undeniable that Armor HUD provides an Unfair Advantage yet it's widely used by the playerbase.

*No Hurtcam* - a mod that stops the screen from shaking when you take damage makes aiming to hit players much easier and makes your aim much more steady due to the lack of having it jarred every time you receive damage. The advantage gained from this mod is very distinct in my opinion, I'm not sure why it would be allowed but it is rampantly used by the player base and most servers in the community allow it freely. Once again, undeniable that No Hurtcam provides an unfair advantage yet is widely considered allowed by the playerbase.

*Saturation HUD* allows you to see how much saturation you have, which is incredibly strong when it comes to healing and avoiding getting popped in PVP. Unlike Armor HUD this is actually something you cannot even see on a Vanilla client, so it allows you to be fully aware of your current saturation & exactly how much you will heal when a Vanilla player would not be aware of that in many circumstances. Once again very obvious that this provides an unfair advantage over a Vanilla player, yet it's considered widely allowed.

Now that I've explained how many of the mods that are widely accepted are quite literally an unfair advantage, I'll explain why I uploaded this video (as well as my Totem Counter mod release video)
Considering other unfairly advantaging mods have been allowed, I don't see why I shouldn't be allowed to submit new mods that have the potential to improve gameplay. Yes, both Totem Counter & Crystal Optimizer are an unfair advantage over a player who does not have them, but not unlike the other mods I mentioned which seem to be widely accepted.
I think it's really silly to cherry-pick why some Unfair Advantages should be allowed, and why some shouldn't be. In my opinion none of it should be allowed, but since it is, I see nothing wrong with contributing my ideas for mods especially since the target purpose of this mod was to help solve an issue that could be much more optimized for laggy players.

*In short, here is my stance on this:*
As I've said before, it's fully up to servers whether they want to allow it's use in gameplay, and I provided 3 disclaimers to only use it where it is allowed (one in the video, one in the description, and one at the download location).

*I think that NO PVP-Enhancement mods/External Advantages should be allowed* - BUT since they are widely considered allowed (whether I like it or not), I believe it's totally OK if I want to contribute with my own ideas to help improve gameplay.
